title: Would physical conditioning improve ones piloting ability
display_title: Would physical conditioning improve ones piloting ability
wikitext:
[]: Would physical conditioning improve one's piloting ability?             Quote Post by G-Force » Wed Nov 14, 2012 6:19 pm This is a question that has been boggling my mind for quiet a while but I always wondered how exactly were the Evangelion's were controlled by the pilots. As we know how the pilots interact with Eva is through the neural links and the levers in the plug. Many have thoerized that its a pilot's thoughts that allow the Eva to perform some of its more complex manuvers and that having a higher synch rate allows for more effectively piloting. The part that got me thinking was looking back into the Eva manga with Asuka's introduction as it showed her being trained in martial arts and having a high level of hand-eye coordination and agility outside of her robot. Does this sort of training yeild any benefit for the Evangelion. Rei too also shows some coordination outside of Eva as was able to land perfectly when falling from the air ducts during the blackout episode. If we are to go with the notion that it's a pilot's thoughts that controls the Eva then does that mean the robots would be limited to a range of motion that the pilot is used to? An example would be if I was never trained in martial arts and I wanted my Eva to do a complicated roundhouse kick. Would my attempts still be clumsy as I have no knowledge in the proper technique in doing so, or does simply knowing the existance of the technique and its existance will grant my the ability to do so? Think Taskmaster from Marvel Comics as an example. Of course the piloting ability of Shinji spits in the face of these questions as he is capable of feats of agility and technique in his Eva while at the same time being very awkward and clumsy. Meaning he could either be the exception to the rule or proof that physical training does not matter.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Wed Nov 14, 2012 6:51 pm That's manga Asuka, I've read most of the manga the martial arts training didn't really help her too much. A higher synch score would allow for a greater range of movement, without the need for physical training. My point is that it takes two to tango, the pilot and the resident soul to achieve a higher performance output. Basically Asuka and Shinji need to open up to their mommy's, but it's very difficult for them. The perfect example would be Asuka in EoE, where she is able to reach a form of berserker mode with kyoko after her big Epiphany.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Nov 15, 2012 5:44 am Don't we know for a fact that physiology doesn't effect sync ratios? It's purely mental.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Nov 15, 2012 2:17 pm Yes, but martial arts training can have all sorts of mental effects. Developing a repertoire of strikes, holds, blocks and counters, for example. Learning to keep a calm, focused mind in a combat situation. Watching for small balance shifts and other movements to anticipate attacks. Developing situational awareness. Increased pain tolerance. Decreased hesitation. And don't discount the physical so quickly. It may not affect piloting directly but it would help decrease likeliness of injury in and out of the plug, makes them more capable of resisting or escaping attack from human adversaries, offers a much-needed stress outlet, and promotes better overall health.

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Thu Nov 15, 2012 2:22 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Don't we know for a fact that physiology doesn't effect sync ratios? It's purely mental.

[]:             Quote Post by G-Force » Thu Nov 15, 2012 2:33 pm Then the main question would be is the range of motion/type of movement the Evangelion does affected by the pilot in some sort of way? What I mean is say I want the evangelion to do a backflip while not possessing the knowledge to do so does me knowing what a flip looks like in real life good enough knowledge or would that require me to simply practice while piloting?

[]:             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Thu Nov 15, 2012 2:39 pm G-Force wrote: Then the main question would be is the range of motion/type of movement the Evangelion does affected by the pilot in some sort of way? What I mean is say I want the evangelion to do a backflip while not possessing the knowledge to do so does me knowing what a flip looks like in real life good enough knowledge or would that require me to simply practice while piloting?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Nov 15, 2012 4:09 pm No no no... you're not getting it. G-Force is asking if the Eva can perform moves that the user doesn't actually know how to do except in the vaguest sense. It walks because the pilot thinks of walking, something they've done lots of times personally... but can the eva do a roundhouse kick if they think to do so, but don't actually have the practiced coordination to do? IOW, are they just suggesting things for the eva or are they actually doing the muscle movement thinking for it? And if the eva's coordination is the limiter, does it matter if the resident soul had such training? Was Yui not just a scientist but a science ninja ?

[]:             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Thu Nov 15, 2012 4:17 pm NemZ wrote: No no no... you're not getting it. G-Force is asking if the Eva can perform moves that the user doesn't actually know how to do except in the vaguest sense. It walks because the pilot thinks of walking, something they've done lots of times personally... but can the eva do a roundhouse kick if they think to do so, but don't actually have the practiced coordination to do? IOW, are they just suggesting things for the eva or are they actually doing the muscle movement thinking for it? And if the eva's coordination is the limiter, does it matter if the resident soul had such training? Was Yui not just a scientist but a science ninja ?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Nov 15, 2012 4:24 pm If you look at what the pilots are shown doing, they quite often adopt some of the poses that their Eva is doing -- Rei in particular in episode 22 IIRC mimics the firing posture.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Nov 15, 2012 4:39 pm RJ, nobody's saying they have to physically do a kick in the plug for the eva to kick (though as Tines has pointed out they do tend to move a little when they can). The question is if the pilot has to be able to perform a kick under normal circumstances (like, say, in a dojo for practice) so that they can think about it correctly with experience and thus tell the eva exactly how to move rather than akwardly tossing it's leg in the air and falling over. To put it another way, could eva01 type on a huge keyboard if Shinji can only hunt & peck? What if he hasn't learned how but Yui was as fast as Ritsuko?

[]:             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Thu Nov 15, 2012 5:14 pm NemZ wrote: No no no... you're not getting it. G-Force is asking if the Eva can perform moves that the user doesn't actually know how to do except in the vaguest sense. It walks because the pilot thinks of walking, something they've done lots of times personally... but can the eva do a roundhouse kick if they think to do so, but don't actually have the practiced coordination to do? IOW, are they just suggesting things for the eva or are they actually doing the muscle movement thinking for it? And if the eva's coordination is the limiter, does it matter if the resident soul had such training? Was Yui not just a scientist but a science ninja ?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Nov 15, 2012 5:44 pm It would have made logistical sense if the pilots underwent physical training of their own, but there's no indication in the show that they do so. The most they entertain the notion is with the mat-controlled video game in episode #09, but even that is just for synchronizing two pilots' reflexes to each other.

[]:             Quote Post by G-Force » Thu Nov 15, 2012 6:07 pm Glad to see we're getting a good discussion out of this topic as I was afraid it was asked in the past. Obvisously physical conditioning is noting going to be extremely as stuff like strength training would be useless. Overall health and stanima training could prove benificial as well as some sort of physical discipline would prove to be useful for dealing with stress and thinking calmly under pressure. Long story short if there was an Eva pilot trained in say Drunken Boxing, that specific knowledge is going to be too useful (though rather comical). Obviously by the pilot training with the Eva the pilot will be used to the momenvents which can explain why the pilots capable of handling multiple types of firearms without actually shooting a real gun themselves. However, let's say in some weird alternate universe NERV actually got Shinji to train some sort of martial arts that emphasized on footwork (say boxing) would those habbits learned through phsyical training, influcence how he would move in his Eva?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Nov 15, 2012 9:48 pm Though it makes sense that knowing how to do something with your own arms and legs helps when it comes to making your Eva do the same things with its arms and legs through the sync system, it probably makes more sense for the Pilots to be training with their Evas, through the simulation bodies or something. So instead of taking martial arts classes in a Dojo, you'd take martial arts classes while synced to your Eva in a simulation. Similarly, instead of forcing the pilots to go through handgun training or other weapons, Shinji trains how to use guns in a simulation while synced to his Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Nov 16, 2012 12:20 am I'd equate the motions in the plug to be the equivalent to mashing really hard on a button or swinging around a video game controller, even though it has no actual impact on the response from the game. In that sense, an Eva is basically a really big, eldritch Wii.

As we know how the pilots interact with Eva is through the neural links and the levers in the plug. Many have thoerized that its a pilot's thoughts that all
